feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: New Ebola Vaccine Could Help With Rapidly Spreading Outbreak (Live Updates)
entry.summary: The vaccine, which hasn’t been tested on humans, would be the first capable of treating the deadly Bundibugyo strain of Ebola.

entry.title: 6 things a neurologist does to keep his brain healthy
entry.summary: Brain atrophy tends to begin in your 30s and 40s, but certain lifestyle changes can slow or even reverse shrinkage.

entry.title: One type of sitting may pose greater dementia risk than another, study suggests
entry.summary: New research suggests the type of sitting matters for dementia risk, with mentally engaging activities linked to a significant reduction in risk.

entry.title: My mom with Parkinson's moved in with my family. Eventually, it became too much to care for her.
entry.summary: Rachel Stein says caring for her mother with Parkinson's at home became emotionally and physically overwhelming for the entire family.

entry.title: What Is 'Practical Optimism'? This Mindset Might Be The Key To Happiness.
entry.summary: If you're looking for a better outlook on life, you might want to try "practical optimism."

entry.title: A sleep-time ‘sweet spot’ is linked to healthy aging, study finds
entry.summary: Turns out 6.4 to 7.8 hours of sleep a night might be ideal. Here are some tips on how to get the “just right” amount.

entry.title: Doctor evacuated from Congo is ‘helpless’ watching colleagues die of Ebola
entry.summary: As Patrick LaRochelle waits in a specialized hospital room in Prague to see whether he has Ebola, his former colleagues in Congo are beginning to die of the deadly disease.

entry.title: 6 Tick Infested Areas In Ohio
entry.summary: These tick infested areas are important to recognize, as ticks carry many possibilties for disease, most commonly lyme disease.

entry.title: These Extremely Common Speech Patterns Could Be Early Signs Of Cognitive Decline, And A Higher Dementia Risk
entry.summary: It's worth knowing about.

entry.title: New High Cholesterol Guidelines for Women and How To Bring Your Levels Down
entry.summary: If your doctor has ever told you to "keep an eye on your numbers," here's good news: The rules around what’s considered high cholesterol have gotten clearer—...

entry.title: DRC is no stranger to Ebola outbreaks. Why isn’t there a vaccine or treatment to help now?
entry.summary: The fast-growing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo came to the world’s attention only a little more than a week ago and is already the third-largest on record. But it’s the 17th outbreak the country has dealt with since the virus was discover…

entry.title: ‘We could have communicated it better.’ Officials admit missteps on Tewksbury Hospital security.
entry.summary: A series of violent incidents at the state hospital raised questions about how to balance treatment with safety.

entry.title: A Common Vitamin Helps Cancer Defend Itself – But Could Also Let Us Fight It
entry.summary: Vitamin B2 (also known as riboflavin) is crucial in keeping many different processes going in the body.

entry.title: What Happens to Your Liver When You Occasionally Binge Drink
entry.summary: Occasional binge drinking can still stress your liver and impact your health over time. Learn how to drink smarter and better protect your liver.

entry.title: A Common Diabetes Drug Is Linked With 'Exceptional Longevity' in Women
entry.summary: A common diabetes drug may hold great potential to help with aging, even if scientists aren't exactly sure why.

entry.title: Toxic plant on Ming dynasty-era surgical tools may be world’s oldest chemical evidence of topical anesthetic
entry.summary: An analysis of residue on centuries-old surgical tools reveals the use of a toxic anesthetic in Ming dynasty-era Chinese medicine.

entry.title: Coloradan tests positive for hantavirus
entry.summary: The Arapahoe County Public Health said the person is recovering well.

entry.title: What happens to your brain when you eat an avocado every day for six months?
entry.summary: Adding an avocado to your daily diet is a trendy health choice, but a new clinical trial shows it won't provide a quick fix for your brain health or protect against cognitive decline on its own.

entry.title: Dozens of hikers suddenly fall sick on California section of iconic Pacific Crest Trail
entry.summary: Roughly a dozen hikers have reportedly fallen ill with a nasty stomach bug after traversing a section of the scenic Pacific Crest Trail.

entry.title: Arapahoe County man recovering after contracting hantavirus; infection not tied to cruise ship cluster
entry.summary: An Arapahoe County man is recovering at home after testing positive for a variant of the hantavirus that is endemic to Colorado, county health authorities said Friday.

entry.title: Helping Children Laugh Can Make Their Brains More Resilient and Learning Easier
entry.summary: Laughter is the best medicine, according to one old adage. Now, new research suggests it boosts child learning and development.

entry.title: How Your Gut Changes When You Eat One Fermented Food a Day
entry.summary: Nutrition experts share what happens to your gut when you prioritize eating one fermented food daily.

entry.title: Rhonda Clark Holman Obituary May 25, 2026
entry.summary: Rhonda Clark Holman, 73, of Lexington, NC passed away at the Hinkle Hospice House, May 25, 2026 after a 5-year battle with cancer. Born June 17, 1952 in Davidson County to Earl...

entry.title: Months trapped together in Antarctic isolation reveal a risk few long missions can afford to ignore
entry.summary: How can people continue to function as a team when they live together in isolation for months on end? A new study led by the University of Zurich shows that loneliness isn't the only challenge posed by extreme environments. Constant physical proximity can als…

entry.title: Skipping meals and irregular eating habits linked to depression symptoms
entry.summary: A nationwide survey reveals that eating meals at irregular times is tied to a higher likelihood of depression. Dietary diversity can cushion this effect, while regularly skipping breakfast amplifies the connection between sporadic eating schedules and low moo…

entry.title: Laughter Rewires Brain Architecture and Lowers Cognitive Load
entry.summary: Laughter restructures the brain, lowers cognitive load, and builds emotional resilience.

entry.title: The human brain accounts for about two per cent of body weight and consumes about twenty per cent of the body's total energy every day — and that consumption barely changes whether you are solving differential equations or staring at a wall
entry.summary: The number that gets repeated in pop-science columns is the 2/20 split (that the brain is 2% of body weight but consumes 20% of energy) — a small organ, enormous appetite — and I think it’s fair to suggest that many people are led to believe that “thinking” i…

entry.title: Deadly fungal storms are now sweeping the US – and spreading a disease few doctors recognise
entry.summary: As the US dries out, giant storms of dust are becoming increasingly common across the southwest

entry.title: A common dietary supplement — omega-3, the kind found in fish oil and flax — has been shown across nearly 4,000 people to reduce aggression by up to 28%, whether it's the heat-of-the-moment kind or the kind people plan in advance
entry.summary: The standard cultural framing of how aggression operates has, for most of the contemporary period, been calibrated to a particular set of explanatory variables. The variables include the various psychological, social, environmental, and circumstantial factors…

entry.title: Exclusive: Trump admin shutting key US researchers out of global virus response talks, documents and sources reveal
entry.summary: Officials responsible for leading US research on infectious disease threats have been barred from speaking directly with the World Health Organization – effectively shutting them out of global discussions on virus outbreaks, according to documents and multip…

entry.title: Even for nonsmokers, a persistent cough may be a sign of lung cancer
entry.summary: NHS screening catches thousands of early cases, but what about those who have never had a cigarette? Dr Mark Porter explains what symptoms to look out for

entry.title: Scientists Confirm Alcohol Causes Widespread Health Damage
entry.summary: Researchers reviewing decades of evidence conclude that alcohol plays a major role in disease and injury, affecting everything from the immune system to the brain and heart.

entry.title: Golden Retriever Wakes up Little Girl & Instantly Makes Her Smile
entry.summary: A golden retriever instantly cheered up a sleepy little girl after running into her room to wake her up with cuddles.

entry.title: A Common Arthritis Drug Appears to Work When Antidepressants Don't
entry.summary: Around a third of people with major depressive disorder don't respond to standard treatments – but an alternative therapy may be hiding right under our noses.

entry.title: 5 Supplements to Avoid for Kidney Health, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Registered dietitians share five supplements that may harm the kidneys when taken in high doses or incorrectly.

entry.title: One Dietary Supplement Was Found to Lower Aggression by Up to 28%
entry.summary: If you're concerned that you act too aggressively at times, you might be able to regain your calm.

entry.title: 3 medical routines that older people may not need
entry.summary: Recently, investigators have taken on questions about common skin lesions that probably don’t need to be removed, a widely used thyroid medication that many older patients can safely discontinue…

entry.title: Common heart drug taken by millions found useless — and possibly dangerous
entry.summary: A massive international study could upend 40 years of heart attack treatment. Researchers found that beta blockers—routinely prescribed after uncomplicated heart attacks—offered no real benefit for patients whose heart function remained normal, despite being …

entry.title: The 10-Minute Mediterranean Diet Breakfast I Can’t Stop Making
entry.summary: A dietitian shares the 10-minute Mediterranean breakfast she turns to when she wants to feel ready for the day ahead.

entry.title: I Almost Died Giving Birth. Now I Fear For My Daughters.
entry.summary: "I repeatedly said something was wrong, but my concerns were dismissed.”

entry.title: Cut fatigue and increase focus with short movement breaks
entry.summary: Sitting all day may be killing us, but taking quick breaks may be the way to save ourselves — and become more productive.

entry.title: California’s ‘Plan B for STIs’ is working — with one exception
entry.summary: Kaiser doxyPEP study is promising against STIs like syphilis and chlamydia, but wasn't effective against gonorrhea.

entry.title: Nightly Sleep Apnea Pill Fast-Tracked For Approval After Latest Trial Success
entry.summary: Nearly 1 billion people across the world are affected by sleep apnea – and that's not including the people sleeping next to those with the disorder.

entry.title: New Study Says 30 Minutes Of Weekly Exercise Is All You Need — But There’s A Catch
entry.summary: Before you take this as gospel, there’s more you should know.

entry.title: 5 Good Signs Your Skin Is Actually Aging Well
entry.summary: For once, take a chance to look for the good in life.

entry.title: Column | I’m a gastroenterologist. Here’s what antibiotics really do to your gut.
entry.summary: Skip the probiotic with your antibiotics, and bolster your microbiome with these three strategies instead.

entry.title: What Researchers Learned About Childhood Gut Health and Rising Colon Cancer Rates
entry.summary: A new study suggests a toxin produced by certain strains of E. coli may help explain why colorectal cancer is rising among younger adults. Researchers found the toxin-linked mutations were much more common in early-onset colon cancer cases, pointing to possib…

entry.title: How NY and NJ are preparing for all the World Cup worst-case scenarios
entry.summary: Hospitals and government agencies have spent the last year preparing for infectious diseases, extreme heat and more.

entry.title: This Prescription-Strength Retinol Starts At Just $13 — And It's Hiding In Plain Sight
entry.summary: Adapalene is prescription-strength retinoid that can tackle everything from acne to hyperpigmentation.

entry.title: I Was Happy to Pitch in When I Learned My Neighbor Needed Help. I Never Could Have Imagined What It’s Turned Into.
entry.summary: I feel bad about what I have to do.
